title: Ensure Constant Names Follow Naming Convention
description: Constants are variables whose value does not change during the runtime of a program after initialization. In order to maintain readability and simplify debugging, it is recommended that constant names follow a consistent and easily recognizable pattern. This rule checks that all constant names match a given regular expression.
recommendation: Use a consistent and easily recognizable pattern for naming constants in your code. Ensure that all constant names match a given regular expression.
impact: Ignoring the naming convention for constants can make the code less readable, as constants and variables are harder to differentiate. This can make the code difficult to maintain across different team members.

checks.message: Rule: Ensure Constant Names Follow Naming Convention
